reviewer: retrospective critique backfill (code-grounded + existing conformance suite), revision 1

rubric.evidence: Real transformers.js generation in a dedicated module Worker (models/nanollava-vlm/worker.js): dynamic import of the pinned TRANSFORMERS_URL, then LlavaForConditionalGeneration.from_pretrained("Xenova/nanoLLaVA", {dtype:{embed_tokens:"fp16", vision_encoder:"fp16", decoder_model_merged:"q4f16"}, device:"webgpu"}) + AutoProcessor + AutoTokenizer. The worker builds LLaVA-style ChatML — a system line + a user turn whose content embeds the `<image>\n` placeholder — via tokenizer.apply_chat_template({add_generation_prompt:true}), tokenises it, runs processor(image), then model.generate({...inputs, do_sample:false, streamer: new TextStreamer(...)}) streams tokens back to the page via callback_function. This is a WebGPU-ONLY demo: worker probeGPU() calls navigator.gpu.requestAdapter() (presence is NOT enough) and createModelLoader is requiresWebGPU:true — on a no-GPU device (and in a no-GPU headless harness) the page shows a LABELLED needs-WebGPU state with enable steps, never a fabricated result. Because the GPU is genuinely unavailable in a no-GPU runner, the real-run output CANNOT be produced there and is NOT faked; the conformance runner records that class as blocked, not pass. Conformance asserts the mechanism: calls-real-inference (source, matches \.generate\() and loads-clean (console-clean) are tested-pass; io-shape-semantic is manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: No canned output — every streamed token + the final answer come from the real generate() call.

rubric.evidence: Expected output is a free-form text generation conditioned on the image + prompt, delivered token-by-token. For nanoLLaVA this is a free-form visual answer to the user prompt, steerable by the system message. The page appends each streamed token to the answer region and reports prompt-token length + generated-token count + latency from the worker's done message; the 'See inside' panel shows the exact constructed prompt (the templated ChatML system+user prompt).

At-a-glance declares the exact ONNX model id (Xenova/nanoLLaVA, linked) as an ONNX build of qnguyen3/nanoLLaVA (also linked), task (image-text-to-text), architecture (SigLIP-400M vision encoder + Qwen1.5-0.5B decoder (LLaVA-style projector)), params/dtype (~1B · decoder q4f16, vision + embeddings fp16), backend (WebGPU required — needs shader-f16), download (~1000 MB), license Apache-2.0. Matches worker.js exactly. Conformance declares-model-id/task/quantisation page-text + declares-backend script tested-pass.

Loads via shared createModelLoader (modelId:"Xenova/nanoLLaVA", runtime:"transformers.js", dtype:"q4f16", sizeMB:1000, requiresWebGPU:true); onReady enables the Run/Ask control. A valid cached current model auto-initialises with no Load click; Download/Re-download/Update + clear-cache surface otherwise. Conformance shared-auto-init-loader (source) tested-pass; auto-init-on-cached manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: modelId keyed on Xenova/nanoLLaVA so the ~1000 MB multi-file cache is tracked for the no-silent-re-download guarantee.

rubric.evidence:
Status is

; the answer region is aria-live=polite so streamed tokens are announced; the shared loader shows download progress + a Retry path. On failure the worker posts {type:error} and the page surfaces it as a readable status. The needs-WebGPU honest state is driven by requiresWebGPU. Conformance live-status-region (dom) + errors-on-the-page (source) tested-pass; honest-progress-retry-offline + webgpu-capability-honest manual/capability.

Every control is real + keyboard-operable: 4 sample thumbs (role=button + Enter/Space), drop/choose file, a system-prompt input (default 'Answer the question.'), the user-prompt input (default 'What is in this image?'), a max-tokens number input (20–512), and Run (enabled onReady). The system + user prompts both condition generation.

No hardcoded caption/answer: the streamed text and readout stats all come from the real generate() call. With no WebGPU the honest needs-WebGPU state shows instead of a canned answer. Conformance no-fake-output is manual-evidenced.

Sample thumbnails carry alt (conformance images-have-alt tested-pass); every button has a text name (buttons-have-names tested-pass); status + streaming answer use role=status / aria-live=polite; light+dark use the WCAG-AA design system. The prompt/system inputs are labelled text fields.

Conformance responsive-desktop + responsive-mobile (no-horizontal-overflow) are the tested assertions; the vlm-grid image + output columns flex-wrap to one column on narrow mobile, the preview image is max-inline-size:100%, the sample strip flex-wraps. parity-both-classes manual-evidenced.
rubric.notes: Mobile cost (~1000 MB q4f16 + a WebGPU requirement) surfaced in at-a-glance.

rubric.evidence: All generation runs in the dedicated module Worker; the main thread only appends streamed tokens (cheap text updates), so INP stays low during a run. Conformance inference-off-main-thread (source: new Worker) tested-pass; inp-stays-low manual-evidenced.

guidanceConsulted.query: break up long tasks keep INP low run heavy work off the main thread in a web worker
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move heavy synchronous work off the main thread and break up long tasks so the browser can service input and repaint; workers + yielding keep interactions responsive.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — generate() + TextStreamer run in a dedicated module Worker; the page thread only appends streamed tokens and reads the final stats.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: worker.js new Worker(type:module) + streamer callback; conformance inference-off-main-thread pass

guidanceConsulted.query: keep INP low, responsive interactions and Core Web Vitals for a heavy on-device workload
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Optimise INP/LCP/CLS: never block the main thread with heavy compute, stream progress, keep interaction handlers cheap.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — heavy decode is off-thread and output streams incrementally, so the interface stays responsive (low INP) even during a long generation.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: streamed tokens posted per-step; no main-thread inference loop

guidanceConsulted.query: accessible live region status updates, accessible names for controls, loading state
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce loading/ready/error via role=status aria-live; give every control an accessible name; semantic HTML + visible focus; mark language for correct shaping.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — role=status aria-live for load/error, aria-live=polite answer region so streamed text is announced, alt on samples, labelled prompt/system inputs, accessible button names.

guidanceConsulted.query: responsive control panel two-column layout without horizontal overflow using flex grid
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use fluid flex/grid with flex-wrap, intrinsic sizing and logical properties; avoid fixed widths that force horizontal overflow.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — image + output columns flex-wrap to one column on mobile, preview img max-inline-size:100%, sample strip flex-wraps; no horizontal overflow.

openQuestions: io-shape-semantic / no-fake-output / auto-init-on-cached are manual-evidenced — a GPU run should capture a real answer + latency to flip them to pass.
At ~1 GB nanoLLaVA is the lightest VLM here — is it a good default 'try a VLM' entry point to route mobile users to over the heavier Qwen2-VL / PaliGemma pages?

followUpGoals.goal: On a WebGPU machine, capture a real VQA answer + streaming latency to satisfy io-shape-semantic / no-fake-output / auto-init-on-cached.
Cross-link the heavier VLM pages to nanoLLaVA as the lightweight entry point for constrained devices.

summary: Retrospective code-grounded critique for nanollava-vlm (Xenova/nanoLLaVA, ONNX of qnguyen3/nanoLLaVA, image-text-to-text, decoder q4f16 / vision fp16, WebGPU-only, ~1 GB, Apache-2.0). A compact LLaVA-style VLM: LlavaForConditionalGeneration.generate over apply_chat_template ChatML (system + user with an embedded <image> placeholder), TextStreamer streaming tokens off the main thread. The lightest VLM in the batch. Honest needs-WebGPU fallback; no fabricated output, genuinely blocked (not faked) in a no-GPU harness. guidanceConsulted: break-up-long-tasks, performance, accessibility, css-layout. Weakness flagged: io-shape-semantic / no-fake-output / auto-init-on-cached manual-evidenced.
